subject: Databases
title: Reading EXPLAIN Output
description: A quick win: understand Reading EXPLAIN Output.

hook.text: EXPLAIN SELECT * FROM users WHERE email = 'ada@test.com'. The output shows: Seq Scan or Index Scan? That's the key.

buildup.text: Seq Scan: reading every row. Index Scan: using an index to jump to matching rows. Index Scan is usually better.

discovery.text: EXPLAIN ANALYZE actually runs the query and shows real timings vs estimates. Essential for debugging.

twist.text: A Bitmap Index Scan reads the index first, builds a bitmap, then reads matching pages. Good for moderate selectivity.

climax.text: Nested Loop, Hash Join, Merge Join — the join strategy matters. EXPLAIN reveals which one the planner chose.

punchline.text: EXPLAIN is your X-ray into query execution. Learn to read it.

quiz.question: What additional information does EXPLAIN ANALYZE provide over plain EXPLAIN?
options: It actually runs the query and shows real execution times vs the planner's estimates
It only shows the index names used
It analyzes the table schema for errors
